title: Return value of setuid family of functions should always be checked
description: Return value of "setuid" family of functions should always be checkedAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalmaintainabilityCode Smellsymbolic-executionUnchecked return value of a function from thesetuid-family might cause unexpected behavior and poses a security risk.Why is this an issue?What is the potential impact?Example program that changes between privileged and unprivileged modeHow can I fix it?More InfoFunctions from thesetuid-family includingsetuidandsetgidare used to change the identity of the caller process. They are used to change privileges for the subsequent actions to be executed. If a call to these functions returns an error that is not checked and handled appropriately, the subsequent parts of the program will execute with unexpected privileges.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Return value of setuid family function not checked. Always verify privilege changes succeeded.
Extended setuid family function return value not checked. Always verify privilege operations.
Standalone setuid family function call without return value verification.
Setuid family function called without subsequent return value checking.
Setuid family function return value explicitly cast to void. Remove void cast and check return value.
Setuid family function return value intentionally ignored. This creates security vulnerabilities.
Setuid family function called without error checking before continuing with output operations.
Setuid family function called without verification before program termination.
